mnemonics: CRUD DOM: Create, Read, Update, Delete DOM nodes
keyPoints: The DOM is a tree-like structure of nodes representing the HTML document
Nodes can be elements, text, comments, or attributes
You can use methods like getElementById, querySelector, createElement, appendChild, and removeChild to manipulate nodes
commonMistakes: Forgetting to wait for DOMContentLoaded before accessing elements
Directly manipulating innerHTML instead of using safer DOM methods
Creating memory leaks by not removing event listeners from deleted elements
